ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301339 covers brewed coffee as a category. The Coffee Traveller is a bulk-format commercial product (96 oz / 8 cups); no exact FoodOn term exists for bulk-format brewed coffee carriers. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKL covers brewed coffee generically. The bulk carrier format is not separately coded in FoodEx2. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731007 represents coffee as a substance. The commercial bulk-format product is not separately coded. Closest parent used.

main_ingredients: brewed coffee (coffee of the week blend)

serving.description: 1 carrier (96 fl oz / approximately 2,839 g total; 8 cups of approximately 355 ml each)
serving.natural_language_portion: One Coffee Traveller carrier provides 8 standard cups (96 fl oz, approximately 2,839 g) of freshly brewed coffee, intended for group or office service.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, coffee, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 171890)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The Coffee Traveller is a bulk-format commercial brewed coffee product from Starbucks. No branded nutrient panel is publicly available for this specific SKU. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed coffee prepared with tap water (FDC ID 171890), which is the closest approved-source match for plain brewed coffee. The product is described as brewed coffee of the week served without additions; therefore plain brewed coffee values are appropriate. Per-100g values are used directly from the USDA entry. Serving-level calculations are based on 2,839 g total (96 fl oz at approximately 29.57 g per fl oz).

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages: black coffee contains no glycemic carbohydrates; GI conventionally assigned as negligible (effectively zero). Reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed black coffee contains no glycemic carbohydrates (carbohydrate_g = 0.0 per 100g per USDA SR Legacy). The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) confirm that black coffee has a negligible glycemic effect. Per the zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value representing effectively zero GI), gi_category is set to low, and glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate content is zero.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (2 mg per 100 g); caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Plain brewed coffee is very low in sodium and does not contribute meaningfully to dietary sodium load; however, individuals with hypertension should be aware that caffeine in coffee can cause a transient, short-lived rise in blood pressure, and moderate consumption (up to 3–4 cups per day) is generally considered acceptable for most hypertensive patients per clinical guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; no impact on postprandial blood glucose when consumed plain
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain brewed coffee contains no glycemic carbohydrates and has a negligible glycemic index, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sugar, syrups, or high-fat dairy; epidemiological evidence also suggests habitual moderate coffee consumption may be associated with reduced risk of Type 2 Diabetes progression.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; contains chlorogenic acids and antioxidants
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain brewed coffee is essentially free of saturated fat and cholesterol, and contains chlorogenic acids and other polyphenolic antioxidants that may confer modest cardioprotective effects; moderate consumption (3–5 cups per day) is not associated with increased cardiovascular risk in healthy adults per current evidence, though individuals with arrhythmias should consult their clinician regarding caffeine intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Coffee Traveller is a bulk brewed coffee product sold by Starbucks, consisting of a convenient carrier box filled with 8 cups (96 fl oz, approximately 2,839 g) of freshly brewed coffee of the week. It is designed for group settings such as office meetings, events, or catering occasions. The coffee is brewed using Starbucks' rotating weekly single-origin or blend selection and is served without milk, sugar, or flavouring additions unless requested separately. Nutritionally, plain brewed coffee is almost entirely water (approximately 99.4 g per 100 g), with negligible energy (approximately 1 kcal per 100 g), minimal protein (0.12 g per 100 g), and essentially no fat or carbohydrate. It contains small amounts of riboflavin (vitamin B2), niacin (vitamin B3), potassium, and magnesium. The glycemic index is effectively zero because there are no glycemic carbohydrates present. From a clinical perspective, plain brewed coffee is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease when consumed without caloric additions. The caffeine content (approximately 40 mg per 100 ml for brewed coffee) should be considered for individuals sensitive to stimulants. In Singapore, the Coffee Traveller is available via field survey from Starbucks outlets and is commonly ordered for corporate or group catering needs.
common_user_queries: How many cups does a Starbucks Coffee Traveller have?
Is the Coffee Traveller suitable for diabetics?
How much does a Coffee Traveller cost in Singapore?

confidence_reason: The Coffee Traveller is a commercial Starbucks product sold in Singapore via field survey. No branded nutrient panel is publicly available for this specific SKU. Nutrient values are adapted from the USDA SR Legacy entry for plain brewed coffee (FDC ID 171890), which is the closest approved-source match. The product is described as plain brewed coffee without additions, making this adaptation defensible. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact Starbucks blend and brewing parameters may produce minor variations from the USDA reference values, and no Singapore-specific or Starbucks-specific nutrient data was available from an approved source.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 171890 (brewed coffee, prepared with tap water) as no branded nutrient panel is publicly available for the Starbucks Coffee Traveller SKU from an approved source. 2. carbohydrate_g is 0.0 per 100g: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for plain brewed coffee; naturally zero as brewed coffee contains no digestible carbohydrates. 3. sugar_g is 0.0 per 100g: naturally zero for plain brewed coffee without additions. 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 per 100g: naturally zero as brewed coffee is a liquid with no fibre passing into the beverage. 5. saturated_fat_g is 0.0 per 100g: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 7. polyunsaturated_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 8. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 9. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 10. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 11. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 12. vitamin_b6_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 13. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 14. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 15. vitamin_e_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 16. selenium_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 17. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is zero. 18. Serving size of 2,839 g is calculated as 96 fl oz × 29.5735 g/fl oz. 19. The product is sold at SGD 37.60 on field survey data from Starbucks One Fullerton. 20. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean market context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the product is sold in Singapore, following Singaporean dish multilingual rules applied to Singapore-market products. 21. monounsaturated_fat_g value of 0.01 g per 100g is reported by USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 171890.
